name: Stripe Invoices
description: Data contract for the Stripe Invoices object (`/v1/invoices` API). Each row is a billing event — the canonical record of revenue billed (and either paid, outstanding or written off). This contract underpins the GAAP-aligned recognised revenue calculation, AR aging, and dunning workflow. Sourced via the Stripe REST API and webhook stream (`invoice.*` events).

domain.name: Finance
domain.description: Revenue, billing, and customer financial relationships.

informationProduct.name: SaaS Revenue Metrics
informationProduct.description: MRR, ARR, ARPU, churn, expansion — the SaaS revenue dashboard.

dataAsset.name: raw.stripe.invoices
dataAsset.description: Every invoice issued by Stripe — open, paid, void or uncollectible — with full status history. One row per invoice per state change.

team.name: Financial Controller
team.description: Business owner — signs off on revenue recognition rules and audit trail.

team.name: Data Engineering
team.description: Owns the Stripe API ingestion job and webhook stream consumer.

team.name: Finance Analytics
team.description: Primary consumer — builds the monthly revenue close and AR aging.

team.name: Collections
team.description: Consumer — works the dunning queue from the open invoice list.

personas.name: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
personas.description: Signs off on the monthly revenue close that uses these invoices.

personas.name: Financial Controller
personas.description: Reconciles Stripe invoice totals against the GL each month.

personas.name: Collections Analyst
personas.description: Reviews the open and past-due invoice list daily.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Stripe-issued unique invoice identifier. Always starts with `in_`.

columns.name: number
columns.description: Human-readable invoice number Stripe generates (e.g. `INV-0042-A`). What the customer sees on their copy of the invoice.

columns.name: customer
columns.description: Foreign key — Stripe Customer ID being billed. Joins to `raw.stripe.customers.id`.

columns.name: subscription
columns.description: Foreign key — the Stripe Subscription that generated this invoice. Null for one-off invoices. Joins to `raw.stripe.subscriptions.id`.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Invoice lifecycle status: `draft`, `open`, `paid`, `uncollectible`, `void`. Drives revenue recognition (only `paid` is recognised cash; `open` is AR; `uncollectible` is bad debt; `void` is reversed).

columns.name: amount_due
columns.description: Total amount the customer owes on this invoice in the smallest currency unit (cents). Set when the invoice is finalised.

columns.name: amount_paid
columns.description: Total amount actually collected against this invoice in the smallest currency unit. May be less than `amount_due` if partially paid.

columns.name: amount_remaining
columns.description: Outstanding balance = amount_due - amount_paid, in the smallest currency unit. Zero on fully-paid invoices.

columns.name: currency
columns.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in lowercase. Matches the customer's currency.

columns.name: tax
columns.description: Total tax collected on this invoice, in the smallest currency unit.

columns.name: created
columns.description: Unix timestamp the invoice was created.

columns.name: due_date
columns.description: Unix timestamp the invoice is due. Drives the dunning workflow once exceeded.

columns.name: period_start
columns.description: Unix timestamp of the start of the service period being billed.

columns.name: period_end
columns.description: Unix timestamp of the end of the service period being billed. Drives the recognised revenue spread.

columns.name: paid_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp the invoice was fully paid (status → `paid`). Null for unpaid invoices.

columns.name: _extracted_at
columns.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the row was extracted from Stripe.

glossaryTerms.name: Recognised Revenue
glossaryTerms.description: Revenue earned in a period, calculated by spreading each invoice's `amount_paid` evenly across (`period_end` - `period_start`) days using GAAP / IFRS 15 principles. Only invoices with status = `paid` contribute.

glossaryTerms.name: Accounts Receivable (AR)
glossaryTerms.description: Total `amount_remaining` across all open invoices. The AR aging report buckets these by days overdue.

glossaryTerms.name: Bad Debt
glossaryTerms.description: Invoices marked `uncollectible` after dunning fails. Written off against the bad debt reserve.

deliveryTypes.name: Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
deliveryTypes.description: Loaded into the `raw.stripe` schema in Snowflake every 1 hour.

deliveryTypes.name: Stripe API
deliveryTypes.description: Source — REST API at https://api.stripe.com/v1/invoices plus webhook events.

trustRules.name: id non-null + Stripe format
trustRules.description: Every row must have a populated `id` matching the Stripe invoice ID format (`in_` prefix).
trustRules.rule: id IS NOT NULL AND id LIKE 'in_%'

trustRules.name: customer FK valid
trustRules.description: Every invoice's `customer` must resolve to a row in `raw.stripe.customers.id`.

trustRules.name: status is a known Stripe value
trustRules.description: Status must be one of the documented Stripe invoice statuses.

trustRules.name: amount balance arithmetic
trustRules.description: amount_remaining must equal amount_due - amount_paid. Drift indicates a bug in the ingestion job or a Stripe data inconsistency.

trustRules.name: paid invoices have zero remaining
trustRules.description: An invoice with status = `paid` must have amount_remaining = 0.

trustRules.name: Paid invoices have paid_at
trustRules.description: Invoices with status = `paid` must have a populated paid_at timestamp.
trustRules.rule: status != 'paid' OR paid_at IS NOT NULL

trustRules.name: Period start before period end
trustRules.description: period_start must be strictly less than period_end.

trustRules.name: Invoice amounts non-negative
trustRules.description: amount_due, amount_paid and amount_remaining must all be ≥ 0. Refunds are recorded on separate Charge objects, not as negative invoice amounts.

trustRules.name: Freshness within 90 minutes
trustRules.description: Latest `_extracted_at` across the dataset must be within the last 90 minutes.

dataSyncs.name: Refresh frequency
dataSyncs.description: Bulk snapshot via the Stripe API every 1 hour; webhook stream applies incrementals continuously.

dataSyncs.name: Webhook event latency
dataSyncs.description: p95 latency from `invoice.payment_succeeded` webhook to row appearing in Snowflake.

dataSyncs.name: Pipeline uptime
dataSyncs.description: Monthly availability target for the invoices ingestion pipeline.

dataSyncs.name: Audit retention
dataSyncs.description: Per finance policy and tax regulations, every invoice row is retained for 10 years.

lineage.name: Stripe Billing Platform
lineage.description: The system of record for all invoices.

lineage.name: Custom Meltano Stripe Connector
lineage.description: Hourly pull from Stripe REST API into Snowflake `raw.stripe` schema.
